name: CloudSword
what_it_does: Cloud security assessment tool for Chinese cloud providers (Alibaba, Tencent, Huawei, Baidu, Qiniu). Enumerates cloud assets (storage buckets, compute instances, IAM users/roles, domains), tests access permissions, hardens bucket policies, and creates honey tokens for intrusion detection. Has a Metasploit-like CLI interface and MCP protocol support via SSE and STDIO modes.
use_cases: Cloud security posture assessment across Chinese cloud providers
Enumerating storage buckets, compute instances, and IAM entities
Testing object access permissions in cloud storage
Automated bucket security hardening (restricting to image-only uploads)
Deploying honey tokens for cloud intrusion detection
not_for: AWS, Azure, or GCP security testing
best_when: You need to assess security posture across Chinese cloud providers (Alibaba, Tencent, Huawei, Baidu, Qiniu) and are comfortable with a Chinese-language interface.
avoid_when: You work exclusively with Western cloud providers (AWS/Azure/GCP) or need English-language documentation and interface.

auth.notes: Requires cloud provider access key ID and secret via environment variables (CLOUD_SWORD_ACCESS_KEY_ID, CLOUD_SWORD_ACCESS_KEY_SECRET). Optional security token for temporary credentials. Credentials are not persisted by the tool.

pricing.notes: Apache 2.0 licensed. Written in Go. Requires valid credentials for target cloud accounts.

performance.rate_limits: Subject to cloud provider API rate limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Documentation is primarily in Chinese. Error handling and message quality not well documented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read operations are inherently idempotent. Hardening operations may not be safely repeatable.
known_agent_gotchas: Interface and documentation are entirely in Chinese
Very early version (0.0.2) - API surface likely unstable
Module coverage varies significantly by cloud provider
Security tool that modifies cloud configurations - high risk if used incorrectly
Credentials passed via environment variables must be managed carefully

security_score_components.security_notes: Community/specialized tool. Apply standard security practices for category. Review documentation for specific security requirements.
